L1.area: Resource downloading and archive extraction utilities

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability (Zip Slip) exists in the ZIP archive extraction logic. When extracting entries from a downloaded ZIP archive, the entry names are not validated against directory traversal sequences, allowing a crafted ZIP file to write files outside the intended target directory.

L3.area: Sample file downloading and ZIP archive unpacking service
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability (Zip Slip) exists in the ZIP archive extraction logic used when downloading and unpacking sample resource files. Crafted ZIP entry names containing directory traversal sequences can cause files to be written outside the intended extraction directory because the resolved output path is not validated to remain within the target folder.

ground_truth.reason: The downloadAndUnpackResource function extracts ZIP entries using the entry name directly joined to the target folder without validating that the resulting path remains within the target directory, enabling a Zip Slip attack to write arbitrary files via crafted ZIP entries.
